title: corydalis aurea subsp. occidentalis
intro: Corydalis aurea subsp. occidentalis, also known as Western golden corydalis or golden corydalis, is a perennial plant native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as berberine and protopine. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, improving respiratory function, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Corydalis aurea subsp. occidentalis can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are commonly used to treat a range of conditions, from anxiety and insomnia to menstrual cramps and arthritis.
